prompt: A rectangular garden has a perimeter of 100 meters. Its length is 50% greater than its width. The owner decides to modify the garden by decreasing its width by 10% and increasing its length by 20%. After the modification, new plants are to be installed at a cost of $2.50 per square meter. However, if the new area exceeds 600 square meters, a 15% discount is applied to the total planting cost. Additionally, a fence needs to be built along one of the new long sides and both of the new short sides at a cost of $15 per meter. Calculate the total cost (planting and fencing) for the modified garden.
